title: Smoky Savory Egyptian Mincemeat Bake
description: A hearty and comforting Egyptian-inspired dish featuring a tangy mincemeat filling, gooey cheese, and a crunchy texture from dehydrated vegetables.
instructions: In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Add the ground beef and cook until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Stir in the tomato paste, dehydrated eggplant, and dehydrated zucchini.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Transfer the filling to a 9x13 inch baking dish.
Sprinkle the crumbled feta cheese and shredded cheddar cheese evenly over the top.
Sprinkle the panko breadcrumbs over the cheese.
Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Remove from the oven and let cool for 5-10 minutes before serving.
Cook ground beef to an internal temperature of 71°C / 160°F (no longer pink in the middle) before serving.
